what_it_does: This file is a channel attachment record that links the attachment identifier att-c96a7d8b to the board item item-b670d167 in the dragnet-development channel, storing only id, channel, attachment_type, target URI, and an added timestamp.
observations: YAML frontmatter only with no body content
References an external board item using a board:// URI in the target field
added timestamp is ISO 8601 with timezone
standouts: Empty body: it is purely a linkage stub
Target uses a board:// URI rather than a local file path
Serves as a join record between an attachment and a board item
conventions: Uses a board:// URI scheme to identify the target
concerns: No contextual information about what the board item is or why the attachment exists
Target cannot be resolved from this file alone
notes: A bare attachment-to-board-item reference stub in the dragnet-development channel that records only linkage and a timestamp.
questions: What does board item item-b670d167 represent?
Who added this attachment and for what purpose?
What content or artifact is being attached to the board item?
purpose_vs_actual: The file acts as an attachment record and its content matches that purpose: it records metadata linking an attachment to a board item.
intention_signals: It appears intended to create a durable, retrievable association between an attachment and a board item within a channel memory system.
novelty: It uses a board:// URI as the target of an attachment record, which is more typical of a board reference than a file path.
contribution: It contributes a persistent attachment-to-board-item mapping with a timestamp in the dragnet-development channel, which a fused system would keep as a linkage record.
summary_for_embedding: This file is a channel attachment metadata record (att-c96a7d8b) in the dragnet-development channel that references board item item-b670d167 via a board:// URI and records an added timestamp.
intention_for_embedding: It was meant to persist the association between an attachment and a board item for channel memory retrieval.
